name: ui-fidelity-audit
description: Verify that a built UI actually reproduces its reference design — and find WHY it doesn't — instead of trusting that it does. The verification step that should run before anyone says 'it matches': it kills the shared-components / same-design-system illusion (shared tokens ≠ shared primitives ≠ shared composites ≠ matching render), refuses to certify parity from source code or commit messages or 'presentation-only port' labels, inverts the burden of proof (a visible difference is a defect until a cited decision proves it intentional), greps the implementation for scaffold/deferral tells (TODO / later-wave / fallback / commented-out features), renders BOTH surfaces side by side — capturing each once into a structured snapshot via a bundled fidelity probe it diffs offline and reuses across the fix loop instead of re-navigating per check — and produces a defect ledger with rendered evidence and a one-line root cause, then hands confirmed gaps to the mockup-align skill to measure and fix. Reuses /design-sync's render-check taxonomy (per-component pass / bad / thin / variantsIdentical, looped until clean) to catch un-wired variants and thin/empty renders a single-state side-by-side misses; the reference may itself be a claude.ai/design design-system project pulled via /design-sync. Sibling to mockup-align (fixes by measuring computed styles), /design-sync (component-library sync + render-check), and spec-validation (checks real-vs-mocked data).
